content: Node.js built-in net.BlockList (available since Node 15) supports CIDR matching natively. Key methods: addSubnet(addr, prefixLength, 'ipv4'|'ipv6'), addAddress(addr, 'ipv4'|'ipv6'), addRange(start, end). check(addr, type) returns boolean. Supports /0 to /32 for IPv4, /0 to /128 for IPv6. IPv4-mapped IPv6 (::ffff:) must be stripped before checking. Works correctly in Node.js 26 (project runtime). No external package needed for CIDR matching.
